hero.title: The Hotel on Široka Ulica
hero.subtitle: Antonio Zerauschek — trader, banker, factory owner in Italian Zara

media.caption: Antonio Zerauschek and Ester Addobbati — the Italian-speaking elite of interwar Zara

media.src: media/docs/Luxardo Maraschino Zara Dalmatia vintage postcard aerial city.jpg
media.alt: Vintage Luxardo Maraschino postcard showing an aerial view of Zara's harbour and rooftops — the Italian Adriatic enclave
media.caption: Zara from the air — the Luxardo distillery on the harbour front, Široka ulica running through the centre. Zerauschek built his hotel on the main street

media.src: media/docs/Difesa Adriatica 1973 Antonio Zerauschek obituary Zara Florence.jpeg
media.alt: Difesa Adriatica obituary for Antonio Zerauschek, 1973
media.caption: Calypso cigarettes — the Manifattura Zaratina Sigarette, acquired from Peristeridis in 1928

media.src: media/docs/Adolfobati family portrait nine Ottilie Piero Vittoria Pietro Ester Nonna caption.jpeg
media.alt: The Addobbati family: Ottilie and Piero seated, with Vittoria, Pietro, Ester, Beatrice, Noemi, Elsa, and Irma — the civic dynasty behind the Ausonia factory and the Zerauschek partnership
media.caption: The Addobbati family — Ester (centre, standing) married Antonio Zerauschek, connecting two of Zara's main investment families

media.src: media/docs/Collage Pieretto Mime Field Marshal Simeone Adelshali handwritten family notes.jpeg
media.alt: Collage of family photographs and handwritten notes — the Zerauschek and Addobbati network in Zara
media.caption: Ford, Firestone, Telefunken, Esso — 'he was keen on new things and no sooner was invented, than he had to have it'

media.alt: Addobbati family group portrait with handwritten Italian caption — the civic families of Zara
media.caption: The communist trial — not a single worker would testify against him. Some were beaten. They still refused

media.src: media/docs/Grave slab Florence Ester Addobbati Zerauschek Antonio Giuliana Zerauschek Rivolta.png
media.alt: Grave slab in Florence for Ester Addobbati Zerauschek, Antonio Zerauschek, and Giuliana Zerauschek Rivolta
media.caption: Florence — exile, confiscation, and the end of two factories. Antonio died in 1973, aged 84
